title: Share of the population in poverty vs. Electoral Democracy Index
subtitle: Poverty is defined as living on less than $10 a day. This data is adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries. The electoral democracy index is by [V-Dem](#dod:v-dem), based on expert estimates, and ranges from 0 to 1 (most democratic).
caption:
Share of the population in poverty vs. Electoral Democracy Index

Poverty is defined as living on less than $10 a day. This data is adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries. The electoral democracy index is by V-Dem, based on expert estimates, and ranges from 0 to 1 (most democratic).

The scatter plot displays a negative correlation between the Electoral Democracy Index on the horizontal axis and the share of the population living in poverty on the vertical axis. Countries with low democracy scores, generally below 0.4, exhibit high poverty rates, frequently clustering between 60% and 90%. Specific nations in this lower-left quadrant include Burundi, Ethiopia, Benin, Laos, and Bangladesh. As the democracy index increases toward the middle range of 0.4 to 0.6, the poverty rates show a wider dispersion, with countries like Indonesia, Georgia, and Peru positioned between 40% and 80%. In the upper-right section of the chart, where the democracy index exceeds 0.8, the data points concentrate near the bottom of the vertical axis, indicating poverty shares below 20%. Nations such as Australia, Chile, Costa Rica, and Bulgaria occupy this high-democracy, low-poverty zone. The color-coded legend distinguishes regions, with purple dots representing African nations predominantly located in the high-poverty, lower-democracy area, while orange and brown dots for North and South America appear more frequently in the lower-poverty, higher-democracy sections. The data is expressed in international dollars at 2021 prices, relating to income or consumption per capita depending on the specific country and year.
